column3: 9*Acclimatize*:✦ *acclimatize* ➜ to get used to a new 11*place, situation or climate*; Arrive two days early in order to acclimatize.; 4*acclimatize to something* ⇒ It takes many months to acclimatize to life in the tropics.; 4*be/get acclimatized to something* ⇒ We haven’t got acclimatized to village life yet.; 4*acclimatize yourself* (to something) ⇒ She was fine once she had acclimatized herself to the cold.;
column4: 9*Distort*:✦ *distort something* ➜ to change facts, ideas, etc. so that they are no longer correct or true; Newspapers are often guilty of distorting the truth.; *distort something* ⇒ to change the shape, appearance or sound of something so that it is strange or not clear; a fairground mirror that distorts your shape;The loudspeaker seemed to distort his voice.

column2: Word Form: ac6*climat*ze; acclimatiz6*ation*
column4: Examples:✦ Allow time for your body to *acclimatize* to the high altitude.; Take a few days to *acclimatize* to the new time zone.; I’m sure that we will *acclimatize* very quickly to the new economic situation.
column5: Synonyms: get used; adjust; adapt; conform
